region_of_origin: India (popularised in Singapore as street food)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Egg Kathi Roll; 'flatbread wrap food product' (FOODON_03315091) is the closest defensible parent class covering a spiced egg filling wrapped in paratha.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pancakes, crepes and similar products — used as closest FoodEx2 analogue for paratha-based wraps; no dedicated Kathi Roll code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Kathi Roll or paratha wrap. A0FJP (pancakes/crepes/similar) is the closest defensible analogue for a pan-fried flatbread wrap.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Egg Kathi Roll or paratha-based egg wrap as of 2024.

main_ingredients: whole wheat or refined wheat flour paratha

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Egg Kathi Roll weighs approximately 250 g, comprising a pan-fried paratha wrapped around a spiced egg filling, typically served as a single-serve street food item.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Egg and Flatbread Wrap (composite estimate); ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — Paratha and Egg components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Egg Kathi Roll as a composite dish. Values are estimated from ICMR-NIN data for paratha (plain, pan-fried with butter) and scrambled egg, weighted by approximate composition (60% paratha, 40% egg filling with spices and butter). Input values from the draft record (energy 180 kcal, protein 6 g, fat 11.2 g, carbohydrate 14 g) are broadly consistent with this composite estimate and are retained with medium confidence. Sodium 240 mg/100 g is plausible given added salt and butter. Micronutrient values (calcium 32 mg, iron 1.2 mg, cholesterol 80 mg, vitamin C 2 mg) are consistent with ICMR-NIN component data and are retained. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and several vitamins are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid and micronutrient profiles for this composite dish are not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central as a single entry.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Chapati/roti (refined wheat flour flatbread) used as closest analogous food.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Egg Kathi Roll as a composite dish. GI of 62 is estimated from the GI of refined wheat flour chapati/roti (GI ~62, medium, per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008), which is the dominant carbohydrate component (paratha made from refined wheat flour). The egg filling contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (62 × 35.0 g carbohydrate in 250 g serving) / 100 = 21.7. Carbohydrate per serving = 14.0 g/100 g × 250 g = 35.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 240 mg/100 g with low potassium offset and low dietary fibre
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a roll and request minimal added salt and butter, as sodium at 240 mg/100 g (600 mg per standard 250 g roll) combined with low estimated potassium (~160 mg/100 g) and low fibre (1.2 g/100 g) provides insufficient cardiovascular offset; adding spinach or bell peppers to the filling can improve the potassium-to-sodium ratio.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat flour paratha with medium GI (~62) and low dietary fibre (1.2 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake to half a roll per meal, request whole wheat flour paratha to lower the GI and increase fibre, and avoid sugar-containing chutneys, as the refined flour base delivers a glycemic load of approximately 21.7 per standard serving with insufficient fibre to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.0 g/100 g from butter and egg yolk, with trans fat 0.2 g/100 g and sodium 240 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should request vegetable oil in place of butter to reduce saturated fat (currently 4.0 g/100 g, contributing 10 g per standard serving) and trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), and should limit sodium intake by reducing added salt, as the combined saturated fat and sodium load is a moderate cardiovascular risk factor at standard serving sizes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, cumin, garam masala, ginger, garlic) present; saturated fat below 5 g/100 g threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Egg Kathi Roll is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to the presence of anti-inflammatory spices in material quantities; however, nightshade ingredients (green chilli, tomato) should be monitored for individual solanine sensitivity, and butter should be substituted with vegetable oil to keep saturated fat below 5 g/100 g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Egg Kathi Roll is a popular Indian street food dish consisting of a pan-fried paratha (a flaky, layered flatbread made from refined or whole wheat flour cooked with butter or oil) wrapped around a spiced egg filling. The egg is typically scrambled or cooked as a thin omelette directly on the paratha, then combined with sautéed onions, green chilli, turmeric, cumin, garam masala, and fresh coriander. The roll is folded or wrapped and served hot, often accompanied by mint chutney, tamarind chutney, or sliced onions. Originating from the Kathi Roll tradition of Kolkata, India, the dish has become widely popular across South Asia and in Indian diaspora communities in Singapore and Southeast Asia. The texture is soft and flaky from the paratha with a moist, savoury egg filling; the flavour profile is warm, spiced, and mildly pungent from the aromatics. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 180 kcal, 6 g protein, 11.2 g fat (saturated fat 4.0 g), and 14 g carbohydrate with 1.2 g dietary fibre and 240 mg sodium. The medium glycemic index (~62) and low fibre content make portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. The dish is vegetarian and halal-suitable when prepared without meat. It is commonly consumed as a lunch, dinner, or snack item.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Egg Kathi Roll as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from ICMR-NIN component data for paratha and scrambled egg, weighted by approximate composition. Input draft values are broadly consistent with this estimate and are retained. GI is estimated from the closest analogous approved-source food (refined wheat flour chapati/roti, GI ~62, University of Sydney GI Database). Medium confidence is appropriate as no exact dish match exists in any approved primary source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (paratha, pan-fried with butter; scrambled egg) and cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central component data. No single approved-source entry for Egg Kathi Roll exists. Input draft values (energy 180 kcal, protein 6 g, fat 11.2 g, carbohydrate 14 g, sodium 240 mg) are consistent with the composite estimate and are retained at medium confidence. 2. Fields set to -1 (data not available): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Reason: detailed fatty acid profiles, full micronutrient panels, and moisture data for this composite Indian street food dish are not available as a single entry in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 3. trans_fat_g is set to 0.2 g/100 g consistent with the input draft, reflecting minor trans fat from butter used in paratha preparation; this is a plausible value for butter-cooked flatbread. 4. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate based on refined wheat flour chapati/roti (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). If whole wheat paratha is used, GI may be lower (~50–55). 5. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03315091 (flatbread wrap food product) and FoodEx2 A0FJP (pancakes/crepes/similar) are approximate matches; no dedicated codes exist for Kathi Roll. 6. Multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are provided as this dish is served in Singapore's Indian street food context. 7. The dish is classified as vegetarian and halal-suitable in its standard preparation without meat. 8.
